description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white and light gray surface, interspersed with patches of brown and tan mineral staining. The rock's surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a natural, weathered appearance. Several sides of the rock display blue spray-painted markings, suggesting it has been tagged or marked for identification or construction purposes. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its mass and size would make it a significant obstacle in an urban environment. The coloration and markings indicate it may have been moved or placed intentionally, possibly as a barrier or boundary marker. Its overall form and texture suggest it is composed primarily of a hard stone, such as granite or quartzite.
description: A large, rough, white and brown rock with blue spray-painted markings and a jagged, weathered surface. It appears dense, solid, and likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows a mostly white, rough surface with brown mineral stains and no visible markings.
description_view_1: This side reveals blue spray-painted markings on a mottled white and brown surface, with more pronounced textural variation.
description_view_2: The rock's surface here is rough and angular, with additional blue spray-painted markings visible on the lighter areas.
description_view_3: This angle highlights a broad, pale surface with brownish discoloration and no visible human-made markings.
attribute_location: urban park or roadside barrier area
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is evidence of intentional placement as a barrier or marked object, likely for urban use.
